input.prompt:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

data.content:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?
data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-05-08T00:57:13.738Z</current_datetime>

We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

input.initialPrompt:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

data.reasoningText:
The user migrated from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP) and is hitting exit code 8 when test assemblies produce zero tests. This is a known behavioral difference — MTP returns a non-zero exit code when no tests are discovered, whereas VSTest treated that as success.

Let me look at their project files to give specific guidance, and also invoke the relevant skill.

data.content:
This is a well-known MTP behavioral difference. **Exit code 8 means "no tests discovered"** — MTP treats this as an error, whereas VSTest returned 0.

You have two fix options:

### Option 1 — CI command line (recommended)

Add `--ignore-exit-code 8` to your `dotnet test` invocation:

```bash
dotnet test -- --ignore-exit-code 8
```

Or set the environment variable in your pipeline:

```yaml
TESTINGPLATFORM_EXITCODE_IGNORE: 8
```

### Option 2 — Project-level fix

If you want every build to tolerate zero-test assemblies without changing CI scripts, add this property to your `.csproj`:

arguments.new_str:
<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- MTP returns exit code 8 when no tests are discovered (e.g., conditional compilation).
         VSTest returned 0. This restores the VSTest behavior. -->
    <TestingPlatformExitProcessOnMissingTests>false</TestingPlatformExitProcessOnMissingTests>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. With MTP, when no tests are discovered (because `INCLUDE_SLOW_TESTS` is not defined, so all tests are compiled out), the runner returns exit code 8 (which means "no tests found"). VSTest returned 0 in this case.

There are a few ways to fix this:

1. **`--ignore-exit-code 8`** on the `dotnet test` command line — tells MTP to treat "no tests discovered" as success.
2. **Environment variable `TESTINGPLATFORM_EXITCODE_IGNORE`** set to `8` — same effect, useful for CI.


3. **MSBuild property `TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests`** — can be set to `0` in the project file to make the runner return 0 when no tests are found, though this requires a newer version.

For their scenario, the cleanest approach is probably using `--ignore-exit-code 8` in the CI pipeline or setting the environment variable, since that doesn't require project file changes.

Exit code 8 specifically means no tests ran, and they can handle it either through the CLI flag, the `TESTINGPLATFORM_EXITCODE_IGNORE=8` environment variable, or by updating MSBuild properties in newer versions. The best fix really depends on whether they want to handle this at the CI level or in the project itself.

arguments.new_str:
<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- MTP returns exit code 8 when no tests are discovered (e.g., conditional compilation).
         VSTest returned 0. This restores the VSTest behavior. -->
    <TestingPlatformExitProcessOnMissingTests>false</TestingPlatformExitProcessOnMissingTests>
